channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Wisconsin's Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Congress is ready to certify Trump's election win, but his Jan. 6 legacy hangs over the day
item.description: Congress plans to convene during a snowstorm to certify the 2024 election results for President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Higher Social Security payments coming for millions of people from bill that Biden signed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday signed into law a measure that boosts Social Security payments for current and former public employees, affecting nearly 3 million people who receive pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and in other public service jobs.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sales of Minnesota blackout license plates exceed 250,000 in first year
item.description: More than a quarter-million vehicles in the state now have the plates featuring a simple design of white letters and numbers on a black background.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Krueger, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers brace for Trump's promised Jan. 6 pardons. Some are urging restraint
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The fourth anniversary of the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol has a new focus as lawmakers brace for the prospect that President-elect Donald Trump may soon pardon many of the more than 1,500 people charged with crimes for their actions related to the riot.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump, Amazon partner to release new film about her life in theaters worldwide
item.description: Amazon Prime has exclusively licensed a documentary film that will give viewers an “unprecedented behind-the-scenes look" at First Lady Melania Trump, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump meets with Italian PM Giorgia Meloni at his Mar-a-Lago resort
item.description: President-elect Trump appeared Saturday at a screening of a documentary with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump appears with Italian Prime Minister Meloni at his Florida club
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump made an appearance Saturday with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, who was visiting his Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's decision to block Nippon Steel takeover creates uncertainty for U.S. Steel workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — By blocking a Japanese company’s takeover of U.S. Steel, President Joe Biden said he was protecting good jobs in the American heartland. He may be putting them at risk instead.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington received the highest US civilian honor
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the East Room of the White House on a particularly frigid Saturday afternoon, President Joe Biden bestowed the Presidential Medal of Freedom to 19 of the most famous names in politics, sports, entertainment, civil rights, LGBTQ+ advocacy and science.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson reveals Trump's wishes on delivering huge policy overhaul in closed-door meeting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson told House Republicans that President-elect Trump wants one large reconciliation bill passed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican Mike Johnson reelected House speaker in dramatic floor vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Mike Johnson narrowly won reelection Friday as House speaker on a first ballot, overcoming hard-right GOP holdouts in a tense standoff and buoyed by a nod of support from President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reminders of Jan. 6 attack fade in the Capitol as Trump retakes power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Inside the Capitol, reminders of the violence are increasingly hard to find. Scars on the walls have been repaired. Windows and doors broken by the rioters have been replaced. And there is no plaque, display or remembrance of any kind.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Funeral proceedings for President Jimmy Carter begin
item.description: A motorcade will leave from Phoebe Sumter Medical Center in Americus, Georgia, to Carter's hometown of Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Outrage as Biden set to award Hillary Clinton, George Soros with Presidential Medal of Freedom
item.description: President Biden will on Saturday award the Presidential Medal of Freedom to former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, left-wing billionaire George Soros, and 17 others at a ceremony at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Speaker Mike Johnson held onto his job, but there are signs of trouble ahead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — This time was supposed to be different. But while House Speaker Mike Johnson on Friday avoided the dayslong ordeal that his predecessor, Kevin McCarthy, endured to become speaker, his relatively swift victory was hardly a unifying moment.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson faces tough road on Trump agenda after dramatic Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) kept his gavel amid a threat from the right flank of the House GOP, but managing the House GOP is not going to get any easier from here.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money criminal case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but indicated he wouldn't be jailed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fight over Florida tuition for undocumented immigrants continues years later
item.description: A Florida senator wants to repeal a law that grants in-state tuition rates to undocumented immigrants. State Senator Randy Fine says he just wants to put Floridians first, but the bill is facing backlash from across the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden awards the Medal of Honor and Medal of Valor to military heroes and first responders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — On Feb. 15, 1951, Army Pvt. Bruno R. Orig was returning from a mission when he found his fellow soldiers under attack in what's now known as the Battle of Chipyong-ni.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden blocks $14 billion acquisition of US Steel by Japan's Nippon Steel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has blocked the nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel — affirming his earlier vow on the presidential campaign trail to prevent the acquisition of Steeltown USA’s most storied steel company.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, JOSH BOAK and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump will be sentenced Jan. 10 in New York case, days before his inauguration
item.description: In a decision Friday, New York Judge Juan Merchan noted that his inclination was to not impose a sentence of incarceration.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Biden says he will block US Steel sale to Nippon Steel
item.description: President Biden announced Friday he would block the sale of U.S. Steel to Japanese-owned Nippon Steel following a yearlong review of the potential acquisition. “We need major U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker race, Trump's second term, Carter funeral: January brings flurry of political events
item.description: New Year's Day ushers in a month of political events and milestones the likes of which Washington hasn't seen in a while.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, NewsNation

item.title: Bill would exempt Tennessee SROs from immigration status reporting requirement
item.description: A new bill sponsored by a Republican senator would shield school resource officers from having to report a student's immigration status to the federal government in hopes of protecting children in schools.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to honor Liz Cheney, Bennie Thompson with top civilian award
item.description: The pair led the congressional committee that investigated the Jan. 6 , 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Capitol control remains in flux after senator’s death, House seat vacated
item.description: Two special elections have been scheduled — one for the House and another for the Senate — and are set to take place two weeks into the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Kirsti Marohn, MPR News
